subject: Product Management
title: Clayton Christensen's Framework
description: A micro-lesson that makes Clayton Christensen's Framework usable.

hook.text: Clayton Christensen was a Harvard professor who changed how the world thinks about why people buy things.

buildup.text: His Jobs to Be Done theory says: focus on the progress a customer is trying to make, not their demographics.

discovery.text: A 'job' has functional, emotional, and social dimensions. People buy drills for holes, but also for feeling competent.

twist.text: JTBD explains why demographic-based marketing often fails: a teen and a retiree might hire the same product.

climax.text: Companies that understand the job outperform those that only understand the customer segment.

punchline.text: Know the job, and the customer finds you.

quiz.question: What is the core idea of Jobs to Be Done?
options: Focus on the progress a customer is trying to make, not their demographics
Segment customers by age and income
